actual_run_inference: WIT-1: the finite/proxy baseline was confirmed on this run's window.
What was tested: Scaled-zero / scaled-lattice equivalence holds within adaptive tolerance over the tested k-range.
This preserves the route the experiment witnesses; it does not prove the base claim or any necessary condition.

expected_signature.expected_value: within adaptive tolerance for every tested k
expected_signature.pass_rule: scaled-zero correspondence holds for every tested k under tolerance

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: Scaled-zero / scaled-lattice equivalence holds within adaptive tolerance over the tested k-range.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: Scaled-zero correspondence is the witness object for NC3 via zero-ensemble equivalence. Without it, the predicate-transport route loses its finite witness.

candidate_lemmas.name: Finite Zero-Scaling Correspondence Lemma
candidate_lemmas.statement: On this run's window, Scaled-zero / scaled-lattice equivalence holds within adaptive tolerance over the tested k-range held within tolerance. Formalizing this as a finite/proxy lemma is the next research step.

disallowed_conclusions: The RH predicate transports exactly under the gauge.
Zero-scaling preserves RH-relevant structure (that requires OBL_EXACT_RH_TRANSPORT).

this is not a proof of NC3
